chunks.text: اافآء ج ،ذ ٧١٥٨ Judgment Sheet IN THE LAHORE HIGH COURT Multan Bench Multan Judicial Department ICA6202اخس Ms Doctor Agro Chemical, نtc VE8U5 Pakistan through Secretary Ministry of Food, Agriculture & Live Stock, Islamabad, ec JUDGMENT Date ofhearing 09.01.2017 Appellants by: Ms نلفk Chاسaش Haider Aفششسسس فش قس Shah Oureshi, Advocates Respondents by: M فنقل Ali Bhatti, Standing Couنكم on behalf 9fruspondant Nu L Rana Muhammad HussaiL Ass15t3n{ ا8عشقڈ General on belalfouesondints No2 l خُلاآفکعمس7ا This single judgment shall decide C.4 N0.201/2009, ICA No,202/2009, ICA.1 ٤ءز No.203/2009, ICA No.204/2009 and ICA No.206/2009 in which similar facts and question oflaw are involved Ihe appellants, who fegistered distributors of pesticides, have challenged order dated 0BU7 2UU9 passtd by learned Single Judge of this Court, MhO i8m155e their constitutional peitions, whereby appellants had challenged the vires of Rule of the Agricultural Pesticidts Rules, 1973 amended through SRO.No.790/2005 dated 05.08.2005 by the Govemnment of Pakistan, Ministry of Food, Agriculture and Livestock The case of appellants is that are registered distnbutors of pesticides and are regulated under Agriculture Pesticides Ordinance, 1971 'the Ordinance" and Agricultural Pesticides 7:wn5 uppcals they beinB 2خۂاخعنھعا Rules, I9T3 'the Rules" As per rule I of the Rules, the appellants Were ruquired to maintain qualified techinical consisting of Entomologist, Plant = and Toxicologist and appellants were fully complying with the above said requiremnents S0nt of Ihe appellants applied for renewal of their distributorship, when they wcre informed tnul throuph Notification $R0 No.21(/)/2004 aled 13.012004, amendment had inserted new sub clause (c)in rle 500- rule Io tne eliect that persons engaged in the business operations of Agnicultural Pesticides in more than Province shall maintain adequately qualified staff of whom at least 1O persons should agricultural graduates POSt ٤4004!05.

اافآء ج ،ذ ٧١٥٨ Judgment Sheet IN THE LAHORE HIGH COURT Multan Bench Multan Judicial Department ICA6202اخس Ms Doctor Agro Chemical, نtc VE8U5 Pakistan through Secretary Ministry of Food, Agriculture & Live Stock, Islamabad, ec JUDGMENT Date ofhearing 09.01.2017 Appellants by: Ms نلفk Chاسaش Haider Aفششسسس فش قس Shah Oureshi, Advocates Respondents by: M فنقل Ali Bhatti, Standing Couنكم on behalf 9fruspondant Nu L Rana Muhammad HussaiL Ass15t3n{ ا8عشقڈ General on belalfouesondints No2 l خُلاآفکعمس7ا This single judgment shall decide C.4 N0.201/2009, ICA No,202/2009, ICA.1 ٤ءز No.203/2009, ICA No.204/2009 and ICA No.206/2009 in which similar facts and question oflaw are involved Ihe appellants, who fegistered distributors of pesticides, have challenged order dated 0BU7 2UU9 passtd by learned Single Judge of this Court, MhO i8m155e their constitutional peitions, whereby appellants had challenged the vires of Rule of the Agricultural Pesticidts Rules, 1973 amended through SRO.No.790/2005 dated 05.08.2005 by the Govemnment of Pakistan, Ministry of Food, Agriculture and Livestock The case of appellants is that are registered distnbutors of pesticides and are regulated under Agriculture Pesticides Ordinance, 1971 'the Ordinance" and Agricultural Pesticides 7:wn5 uppcals they beinB 2خۂاخعنھعا Rules, I9T3 'the Rules" As per rule I of the Rules, the appellants Were ruquired to maintain qualified techinical consisting of Entomologist, Plant = and Toxicologist and appellants were fully complying with the above said requiremnents S0nt of Ihe appellants applied for renewal of their distributorship, when they wcre informed tnul throuph Notification $R0 No.21(/)/2004 aled 13.012004, amendment had inserted new sub clause (c)in rle 500- rule Io tne eliect that persons engaged in the business operations of Agnicultural Pesticides in more than Province shall maintain adequately qualified staff of whom at least 1O persons should agricultural graduates POSt ٤4004!05. Through another SRO No.790/2005 d#ted 05.08.2005_ clause tc) of Rule 9, sub rule was replaced by un umended clause in the following terIns: "(c who mumniai uutqualely qalijied sayy a! ihc places where 0PBr0I0/15 caried Uul, ui per strengin given Jor each province n fhc Jollowing numety: Provnt "'3}40 اذانن ان J Agriculiwal Grdduutes Provice ٢Sinl ٧٧5٢ 04 Agricuhilaral Graduates 5٥٧٦٤٤ 9)MW#FP |P5I 02 Aigrtculiural Graduafes Provinee oy Balochistan Iuu7 02 Agricuawral Gradhults The constitutional petitions filed by appellants challenging the VIF5 Uf umendment in rule e) ufthe Rules made through SRO No.790(/)/2005 ''''٢ dismissed the leamed Single Judge Chambers and said order is under challenge in the titled appeals During the pundency of the appeals through SR0 No.63T(IN2oI dated 2OO62OI5 the number of required agriculural graduates for Punjab Icuuccd following amendment in nle 9S)te)of the Rules: " e) Uvlo mlulniuin auequalely qualijied stayj ar the places operations are CUFFiU OMl 08 per sengIh giveM Jor uth provlnice In Ihe _ Jollowing numety: Province @f Punjab ul least 05 Agricuiwral Gradaales The contention of leamed counsel for appellants is Ihat they only the distibutors and not importers, therefore, the above szid SRO 5١٨١T Pathologisl making ٧٤٢٥ ١٠ فالۃ ولاباعفطCا is not applicable to their case and even otherwise it is contended that SRO dated 05.08.2005 is ultra vie Of Anicles of the Constitution of Islamic Republic of Pakistan 19T3; that Govemment was not authorized to issue such an order in ters of Section 29 of the Ordinance and that impugned SRO is also discriminatory as in other Provinces less number of agricultural graduates r٤د required to be employed On the other hand leamed Law Officers have opposed the titled Intra Court Appeals on the gruund that framing of rules is prerogative of the Federal Govemnment and the Legislature; that keeping V1٦ the different population and cultivatable area, diffcrent number of field staffhas been required to be employed in the four Provinces thus appellants carnot claim discrimination It 15 further Ihat Legislature in iis intent has laid classification which in the facts and circumstances is reasonable and covered by Article 25 of the Constitution of te Islamie Republic of Pakistan, 1973.

Coifon Quality Inernutional Government qy Pujub has merely stated that similar constitutional petitions have been dismissed by this Court without refening Ihe reasoning laid down in those judgments reproducing any matter that has been decided by those juugments Therefore, the order of the leamed Single Judge being devoid of reasoning not Sustainable The leamed Standing Counsel relied upon Ihe decision of (1.C.A No.79/2008 |.P.No,3519/2008 دناار ت Pesticiuer unulher Federanon oy Puhisiun & uthers decide 21.04.2010 Multn Bench of this Coun defiend the order of leamed Single Judge of this Court The obaervutions mude by this Cuun in the ubove said Intra Cout Apptal reproduced below: Ic JI٥٧8"4 SRO Ufted 29 of the Agriculwrol Oruinonce I97I which isedf makes incumbent upon Inc Pesticiaer داس0 Appell
